---
title: "gbif_sketch"
author: "Robert McGuinn"
date: "2026-06-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r gbif-analysis, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Load GBIF package
library(rgbif)

# 1. Create a WKT (Well-Known Text) bounding box to query the GBIF API
# GBIF requires spatial searches to be formatted this way
aoi_bbox <- st_bbox(aoi_polygon)
wkt_geom <- sprintf("POLYGON((%f %f, %f %f, %f %f, %f %f, %f %f))",
                    aoi_bbox["xmin"], aoi_bbox["ymin"],
                    aoi_bbox["xmax"], aoi_bbox["ymin"],
                    aoi_bbox["xmax"], aoi_bbox["ymax"],
                    aoi_bbox["xmin"], aoi_bbox["ymax"],
                    aoi_bbox["xmin"], aoi_bbox["ymin"])

# 2. Download GBIF data within the bounding box
# Limit set to 1000 for speed; increase if you need a massive dataset
gbif_res <- occ_search(
  geometry = wkt_geom, 
  hasCoordinate = TRUE,
  # datasetKey = "df8e3fb8-3da7-4104-a866-748f6da20a3c", # Forces DSCRTP to only return this dataset
  depth = "25,10000",
  limit =100000
)
gbif_df <- gbif_res$data

##### add the names ##### 
# 1. Get a list of unique hosting organization keys (ignoring NAs)
unique_org_keys <- unique(na.omit(gbif_df$hostingOrganizationKey))

# 2. Ping the GBIF registry (using a safe loop to guarantee matching row counts)
# Pre-allocate a character vector of the exact right length
org_names <- character(length(unique_org_keys)) 

for (i in seq_along(unique_org_keys)) {
  org_names[i] <- tryCatch({
    # Fetch the organization info from GBIF
    org_info <- rgbif::organizations(uuid = unique_org_keys[i])
    
    # Safely extract the title depending on how the GBIF API structured the response
    if (!is.null(org_info$data$title)) {
      org_info$data$title
    } else if (!is.null(org_info$title)) {
      org_info$title
    } else {
      "Unknown Organization"
    }
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    # If the API times out or fails, return a default string
    return("Unknown Organization")
  })
}

# 3. Create a lookup table (This will now perfectly match 9 rows to 9 rows)
org_lookup <- data.frame(
  hostingOrganizationKey = unique_org_keys,
  HostingOrganizationName = org_names,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# 4. Join the actual names back to your main dataset
gbif_df <- gbif_df %>%
  left_join(org_lookup, by = "hostingOrganizationKey")

# 4. Convert to sf and intersect with the EXACT polygon
# (The API queries a bounding box, so we trim off the corners here)
gbif_sf <- st_as_sf(gbif_df, 
                    coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"), 
                    crs = 4326, 
                    remove = FALSE)

gbif_sf <- st_transform(gbif_sf, crs = st_crs(aoi_polygon))
gbif_aoi <- st_intersection(gbif_sf, aoi_polygon)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Output: Depth Summary by Species (GBIF)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
gbif_depth_summary <- gbif_aoi %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(scientificName) %>%
  summarize(
    TotalOccurrences = n(),
    Count = sum(individualCount, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

kable(gbif_depth_summary, caption = "GBIF Data: Summary of Depths per Species in the AOI")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Output: Expedition Summary (GBIF)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
gbif_expedition_summary <- gbif_aoi %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(hostingOrganizationKey, year) %>%
  summarize(
    TotalOccurrences = n(),
    UniqueSpeciesCount = n_distinct(scientificName),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year)

kable(gbif_expedition_summary, caption = "GBIF Data: Expedition Summary within the AOI")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Output: Static Map (GBIF)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Using green to distinguish GBIF data from your previous blue dataset
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = us_border, fill = "gray95", color = "gray50") +
  
  # All GBIF points fetched (inside the bounding box)
  geom_sf(data = gbif_sf, color = "gray70", size = 1.0, alpha = 0.5) +
  
  geom_sf(data = aoi_polygon, fill = NA, color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  # GBIF points confirmed inside the polygon (highlighted green)
  geom_sf(data = gbif_aoi, color = "forestgreen", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(aoi_bbox["xmin"] - zoom_buffer, aoi_bbox["xmax"] + zoom_buffer), 
    ylim = c(aoi_bbox["ymin"] - zoom_buffer, aoi_bbox["ymax"] + zoom_buffer),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  annotation_north_arrow(
    location = "tr", which_north = "true", 
    pad_x = unit(0.2, "in"), pad_y = unit(0.2, "in"),
    style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering
  ) +
  annotation_scale(
    location = "bl", width_hint = 0.3, text_cex = 0.9
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "GBIF Occurrences: Inside vs. Outside AOI",
    subtitle = "Red border indicates the AOI; Green points intersect the AOI, Gray points are outside the AOI",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dashed")
  )
```
